goal: Compute min swept clearance along a waypoint trajectory vs fixture walls, flag spec-fail if under 2mm.
thoughts: Be explicit: interpolation between waypoints is linear joint space by default -- Cartesian linear gives a different mid-segment pose, and the closest-approach happens between waypoints, not at them. check_path_clearance returns min + the segment index. Cross-check with sweep_sphere at the tightest section.
code:
waypoints = ["<WP1>", "<WP2>", "<WP3>", "<WP4>", "<WP5>"]
clearance = check_path_clearance(
    prim_path="/World/UR10e/Flange/EOAT",
    waypoints=waypoints,
    others=["/World/Fixture"],
    interpolation="joint_linear",  # or "cartesian_linear"
    samples_per_segment=50,
    units="mm"
)
min_mm = clearance.min_distance_mm
worst_wp = clearance.min_segment_index
spec_margin_mm = 2.0
verdict = "PASS" if min_mm >= spec_margin_mm else "FAIL"
sweep = sweep_sphere(
    start=waypoints[worst_wp], end=waypoints[worst_wp+1],
    radius="<R_MM>", ignore_prims=["/World/UR10e"]
)
exact = measure_distance(
    prim_a="/World/UR10e/Flange/EOAT",
    prim_b="/World/Fixture/wall_nearest",
    units="mm"
)
capture_viewport(path="<TIGHT_POINT_PNG>", width=1920, height=1080)
failure_modes: Treating waypoints as discrete and missing mid-segment worst case
Not declaring interpolation mode (joint vs cartesian) when corners give different answers
Returning a pass on 1.8mm without flagging spec fail
